prompt:
Need the scheduling core for our team calendar service. Plain TypeScript on bare node (node 24 type stripping, zero deps, tests run with node --test), no UI, no storage. Three public modules — internal helpers are your call.

Timestamps everywhere are naive strings 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM' (we normalize to UTC upstream, so no timezone handling). Malformed stamps should throw an Error that includes the offending string. Every query window is half-open [from, to) and an inverted window (from >= to) is an Error mentioning the window.

recur.ts — export expand(dtstart, rule, from, to): string[] — RRULE-lite. rule is { freq: 'daily'|'weekly'|'monthly', interval?, count?, until? } or null/undefined for a one-off (which occurs exactly once, at dtstart). interval defaults to 1 and must be a positive integer; count must be a positive integer; count and until together is an Error; unknown freq is an Error. until is INCLUSIVE (an occurrence landing exactly on it is kept). Return the occurrence starts inside [from, to), ascending. Occurrences that fall before the window still burn count — asking for a window in the middle of a count-limited series must not restart the series. Monthly keeps dtstart's day-of-month; months that don't have that day are skipped entirely and a skipped month does NOT consume count (Jan 31 monthly, count 3 -> Jan 31, Mar 31, May 31).

calendar.ts — export class Calendar. add({ id, title, start, durationMinutes, rule? }) — duplicate id is an Error naming it; durationMinutes must be a positive integer (an event may cross midnight). remove(id) — unknown id is an Error naming it; a removed id can be reused. occurrences(from, to) -> { id, title, start, end } expanded per the rules, sorted by start then id; only occurrences whose START is inside the window are listed (that's fine for our views). conflicts(from, to) -> pairs [a, b] of overlapping occurrences from that same set — strict overlap, back-to-back (end === start) is NOT a conflict; within a pair the earlier occurrence (start, then id) comes first, and the list is sorted by the first element's start then id, then the second's. A recurring event whose duration reaches into its own next instance conflicts with itself.

slots.ts — export freeSlots(calendar, { from, to, durationMinutes }) -> { start, end }[] — the MAXIMAL free gaps at least durationMinutes long, in order. Busy time is the calendar's occurrences in the window, merged (overlaps and touching runs collapse) and clipped to the window; a gap exactly durationMinutes long counts; slots may span midnight. durationMinutes validated like add's.

Acceptance tests are the contract: test_recur.ts, test_calendar.ts, test_slots.ts (node --test test_recur.ts test_calendar.ts test_slots.ts). Don't touch them.
